topic: Preference-Based Learning in Audio Applications: A Systematic Analysis
topic_definition: Stage 1 observable projection of the original paper's eligibility. Use title+abstract evidence for preference-learning-in-audio fit. Keep source-faithful boundaries only: ranking/A-B style preference signals (or RL loop for an audio model), audio-domain applicability (including multimodal works including audio), and exclusion of preference-only-for-evaluation studies. Avoid adding extra hard gates not stated in the original paper.
summary: Stage 1 title/abstract projection of source-faithful Stage 2 criteria; no additional operational hardening.

summary_topics.description: Preference-Based Learning in Audio Applications: A Systematic Analysis

required.criterion: Title/abstract indicates preference-learning evidence in audio, such as ranking or A/B comparison of two or more audio clips.
Numeric ratings count as preference evidence only when title/abstract indicates conversion to rankings or comparative preference signals.
Papers without explicit comparisons remain eligible when title/abstract indicates an RL training loop for an audio model.
Paper is applied within the audio domain. Multimodal works including audio are treated as within the audio domain.
Paper is not a survey or review article.

exclusion_criteria.criterion: Studies that use preferences solely for evaluation, with no learning component, are excluded.
Title/abstract clearly indicates the study is not applied within the audio domain.
Survey or review articles are excluded.
